result:
I’d change how people experience “other people.”

Not laws, not tech, not even resources—those all flow downstream from something deeper. I’d make it so that, for just a few moments in every interaction, you could genuinely feel the inner world of the person in front of you. Not read their thoughts, not lose yourself—just directly sense that they’re as real, as complicated, as fragile and driven as you are.

A lot of what goes wrong in the world depends on distance—emotional distance, psychological distance, the ability to flatten someone into an idea or a category. Close that gap, even a little, and cruelty becomes harder to sustain, indifference gets uncomfortable, and cooperation stops feeling like a sacrifice.

It wouldn’t magically fix everything—people would still disagree, compete, even hurt each other—but it would change the baseline. Fewer throwaway harms. More hesitation before damage. More moments where someone thinks, “oh… right. you’re like me.”

That shift would ripple into everything else.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
